title: Smoky Pickled Greek Eggplant Salad
description: A juicy, umami-packed salad that balances smoky roasted eggplant with tart pickle notes and a splash of dry sherry, perfect for a low-sodium Greek-inspired lunch or dinner.

ingredients.name: olive oil
ingredients.notes: divided: 1 Tbsp for roasting, 1 Tbsp for dressing

instructions: Slice eggplant into ½‑inch thick discs; sprinkle with ½ tsp salt, let rest 20 min to draw out bitterness, rinse and pat dry.
Toss eggplant slices with 1 Tbsp olive oil and roast 15 min, flipping halfway.

Meanwhile, combine 1 cup water, ¼ cup apple cider vinegar, ½ tsp salt, 1 tsp dried oregano, 1 tsp lemon zest, 1 Tbsp lemon juice, and ¼ cup dry sherry in a saucepan; bring to a boil, then cool to room temperature.
Slice cucumber thinly; add to the cooled brine with ¼ cup olives; refrigerate 30 min to infuse flavors.
Transfer roasted eggplant to a bowl; drizzle remaining 1 Tbsp olive oil, add 2 Tbsp red wine vinegar, and a pinch of pepper; toss gently.
Add the pickled cucumber and olives to the eggplant; fold in crumbled feta and toasted pine nuts.
Sprinkle chopped fresh oregano; adjust seasoning with a pinch of low‑sodium salt if needed.
Let the salad sit at room temperature 10 min to meld flavors; serve chilled or at room temperature, optionally with warm pita or crusty bread.
